questionText: In your organization's financial accounting, do you identify spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition?
questionKo: 재무제표 내에 귀사의 기후변화계획에 부합한 비용 / 매출액을 파악하고 있습니까 ?
sectorTags: Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: Organizations need to be aware of whether their spending and revenue is supporting their climate transition. Basing such an assessment on activities, projects, or assets defined as sustainable by a sustainable finance taxonomy can inform progress being made on their commitment to mitigate and adapt to climate change and enhance its credibility.

columns.text: Identification of spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition
options: No, but we plan to in the next two years
No, and we do not plan to in the next two years
columns.simple_help: Select "Yes, we identify the alignment with a sustainable finance taxonomy" if you identify alignment with the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities.
columns.textKo: Identification of spending/매출 that is aligned with 귀사의 climate transition
optionsKo: No, but we plan to in the next two years
No, 및 we do not plan to in the next two years
columns.simple_helpKo: "Yes, we identify the alignment with a sustainable finance taxonomy" 만약 you identify alignment with the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities.을/를 선택하십시오.

columns.text: Methodology or framework used to assess alignment with your organization's climate transition
columns.textKo: 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition

columns.text: Indicate the level at which you identify the alignment of your spending/revenue with a sustainable finance taxonomy
options: At both the organization and activity level
columns.simple_help: If reporting alignment with the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, select "At both the company and activity level".
columns.textKo: the level at which you identify the alignment of your spending/매출 with a sustainable finance taxonomy을/를 표시하십시오.
optionsKo: At both the organization 및 activity level
columns.simple_helpKo: 만약 reporting alignment with the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, select "At both the company 및 activity level".

guidance.text:
Identification of spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition (column 1) 
Select "Yes" if, in your financial statements, you identify spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition, as assessed using any methodology or framework to track the progress of your climate transition.
Note that if your organization has a climate transition plan as indicated in 5.2, it is considered best practice to include within your climate transition plan details of the methodology/framework used to assess the alignment of your spending/revenue with your transition.
It is up to each organization to determine what is considered to be aligned with your organization's climate transition. For example:
Revenue derived from the sale of low-carbon products or services as defined via recognized taxonomies or methodologies.
Spending (e.g., CAPEX or OPEX) on the implementation of emissions reduction initiatives and/or investment in new low-carbon assets or projects.
You will have the opportunity to provide further details in the subsequent questions.
Methodology or framework used to assess alignment with your organization's climate transition (column 2) 
This column only appears if "Yes" is selected in column 1 "Identification of spending/revenue that is aligned with your organization's climate transition".
Select "A sustainable finance taxonomy" if you use a sustainable finance taxonomy to assess the alignment of your organization's spending/revenue with your climate transition (e.g., the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities).
Select "Other methodology or framework" if you use a different methodology of assessment, as disclosed in your climate transition plan (e.g., a self-assessment of your financial planning against time bound KPIs outlined in your transition plan). You may also select this option if you do not have a climate transition plan but are using a bespoke methodology to assess the alignment of your organization's spending/revenue with your climate transition.
If you use a sustainable finance taxonomy to assess some of your organization's activities, projects, or assets, and another methodology or framework to assess those that are not covered by the sustainable finance taxonomy, select both options. For instance, if you are disclosing against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, you may use a bespoke methodology for assessing the alignment of activities not eligible under the taxonomy (e.g. wholesale and retail trade, manufacturing of food, beverages and tobacco etc.).
Indicate the level at which you identify the alignment of your spending/revenue with a sustainable finance taxonomy (column 3) 
This column only appears if "A sustainable finance taxonomy" is selected in column 2 "Methodology or framework used to assess alignment with your organization's climate transition".
Select "At the organization level only" if you wish to disclose alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy, but the taxonomy does not require alignment information to be provided at the activity level. For example, you should select this option if the taxonomy requires a breakdown at project or asset level but not at activity level, or if the taxonomy requires just an overall figure at organization/group level. See "Explanation of terms" for more information.
Organizations that wish to disclose alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities should select "At both the organization and activity level".
guidance.textKo: Identification of spending/매출 that is aligned with 귀사의 climate transition (열 1) Select "예" 만약, in your 재무제표, you identify spending/매출 that is aligned with 귀사의 climate transition, as assessed using 하나 이상 방법론 또는 framework to track the progress of your climate transition. Note that 만약 귀사 has a 기후 전환계획 as indicated in 5.2, it is considered best practice to include within your 기후 전환계획 details 의 방법론/framework used to assess the alignment of your spending/매출 with your transition. It is up to each organization to determine what is considered to be aligned with 귀사의 climate transition. For example: 매출 derived from the sale of low-carbon products 또는 services as defined via recognized taxonomies 또는 methodologies. Spending (e.g., 자본지출(CAPEX) 또는 운영비용(OPEX)) on the 이행 of emissions reduction 이니셔티브 및/또는 investment in 신규 low-carbon 자산 또는 projects. You will have the opportunity to provide further details in the subsequent 문항. 방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition (열 2) This 열 only appears 만약 "예" is 선택 in 열 1 "Identification of spending/매출 that is aligned with 귀사의 climate transition". Select "A sustainable finance taxonomy" 만약 you use a sustainable finance taxonomy to assess the alignment of 귀사의 spending/매출 with your climate transition (e.g., the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities). Select "Other 방법론 또는 framework" 만약 you use a different 방법론 of assessment, as 공개 in your 기후 전환계획 (e.g., a self-assessment of your 재무계획 against time bound KPIs outlined in your 전환계획). You may also select this option 만약 you do not have a 기후 전환계획 but are using a bespoke 방법론 to assess the alignment of 귀사의 spending/매출 with your climate transition. 만약 you use a sustainable finance taxonomy to assess some of 귀사의 activities, projects, 또는 자산, 및 another 방법론 또는 framework to assess those that are not covered by the sustainable finance taxonomy, select both options. For instance, 만약 you are disclosing against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities, you may use a bespoke 방법론 for assessing the alignment of activities not 획득 가능 under the taxonomy (e.g. wholesale 및 retail trade, manufacturing of food, beverages 및 tobacco etc.). Indicate the level at which you identify the alignment of your spending/매출 with a sustainable finance taxonomy (열 3) This 열 only appears 만약 "A sustainable finance taxonomy" is 선택 in 열 2 "방법론 또는 framework used to assess alignment with 귀사의 climate transition". Select "At the organization level only" 만약 you wish to disclose alignment against a sustainable finance taxonomy, but the taxonomy does not require alignment information to be 입력 at the activity level. For example, you 해야 합니다 select this option 만약 the taxonomy requires a breakdown at project 또는 asset level but not at activity level, 또는 만약 the taxonomy requires just an overall figure at organization/group level. See "설명 of terms" for more information. Organizations that wish to disclose alignment against the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities 해야 합니다 select "At both the organization 및 activity level".

guidance.text:
Sustainable finance taxonomies are instruments to support the redirection of financial flows towards environmentally (and socially) sustainable activities. While a coherent disclosure system with internationally agreed standards and taxonomies is needed to ensure effective data access, analysis, and use by the data users, CDP's 2025 strategy has highlighted that a harmonized taxonomy landscape is crucial to support the investment decisions of capital market actors and help to prevent greenwash, as laid out in CDP's policy brief on sustainable finance taxonomies. 
As a global environmental disclosure system, CDP aims to accelerate the implementation of sustainable finance taxonomies at scale. This is reflected in the questions in this module, asking organizations to report the alignment of their business operations and financial accounting with sustainable finance taxonomies, such as the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities. 
These questions are taxonomy-agnostic, allowing organizations to provide information on alignment with any sustainable finance taxonomy. However, additional guidance is provided for organizations choosing to report under the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities. The following (non-exhaustive) list of sources contains references to detailed taxonomy disclosure guidance developed by the EU Commission and the Technical Expert Group on Sustainable Finance (TEG): 
Regulation (EU) 2020/852 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 18 June 2020 ( EU Taxonomy Regulation) 
Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/2178 of 6 July 2021 ( Disclosure Delegated Act (Annexes 1-5)) 
Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/2139 of 4 June 2021 ( Climate Delegated Act ) - for technical screening criteria and DNSH criteria for taxonomy eligible activities 
Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/1214 of 9 March 2022 ( Complementary Delegated Act ) - for reporting on nuclear and fossil gas activities 
Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2485 of 27 June 2023 ( 2023 amendment to the Climate Delegated Act ) - for the 12 newly added activities eligible under the EU Taxonomy 
EU Commission FAQs - on how the EU Taxonomy will work in practice 
EU Commission FAQs - for reporting eligibility of activities and assets 
EU Commission FAQs - on disclosures under Article 8 of the EU Taxonomy 
EU Commission FAQs - on the interpretation and implementation of Article 8 of the EU Taxonomy 
EU Taxonomy FAQs - on the interpretation and implementation of technical screening criteria and Do no significant harm (DNSH) criteria. 
TEG Taxonomy Final Report 
TEG Taxonomy Final Report - Technical Annex 
EU Taxonomy compass - for a visual representation of the contents of the EU Taxonomy
guidance.textKo: Sustainable finance taxonomies are instruments to support the redirection of financial flows towards environmentally (and socially) sustainable activities. While a coherent 공시 system with internationally agreed 기준 및 taxonomies is needed to ensure effective data access, analysis, 및 use by the 데이터 사용자, CDP's 2025 전략 has highlighted that a harmonized taxonomy landscape is crucial to support the investment decisions of capital market actors 및 help to prevent greenwash, as laid out in CDP's 정책 brief on sustainable finance taxonomies. As a global environmental 공시 system, CDP aims to accelerate the 이행 of sustainable finance taxonomies at scale. This is reflected in the 문항 in this module, asking organizations to report the alignment 의ir business operations 및 financial accounting with sustainable finance taxonomies, such as the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities. These 문항 are taxonomy-agnostic, allowing organizations to provide information on alignment with 하나 이상 sustainable finance taxonomy. However, additional guidance is 입력 for organizations choosing to report under the EU Taxonomy for Sustainable Activities. The following (non-exhaustive) list of sources contains references to detailed taxonomy 공시 guidance developed by the EU Commission 및 the Technical Expert Group on Sustainable Finance (TEG): Regulation (EU) 2020/852 의 European Parliament 및 의 Council of 18 June 2020 (EU Taxonomy Regulation) Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/2178 of 6 July 2021 (공시 Delegated Act (Annexes 1-5)) Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/2139 of 4 June 2021 (Climate Delegated Act) - for technical screening 기준 및 DNSH 기준 for taxonomy 획득 가능 activities Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/1214 of 9 March 2022 (Complementary Delegated Act) - for reporting on nuclear 및 fossil gas activities Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2485 of 27 June 2023 (2023 amendment to the Climate Delegated Act) - 에 대한 12 신규ly added activities 획득 가능 under the EU Taxonomy EU Commission FAQs - on how the EU Taxonomy will work in practice EU Commission FAQs - for reporting eligibility of activities 및 자산 EU Commission FAQs - on 공시s under Article 8 의 EU Taxonomy EU Commission FAQs - on the interpretation 및 이행 of Article 8 의 EU Taxonomy EU Taxonomy FAQs - on the interpretation 및 이행 of technical screening 기준 및 Do no significant harm (DNSH) 기준. TEG Taxonomy Final Report TEG Taxonomy Final Report - Technical Annex EU Taxonomy compass - for a visual representation 의 contents 의 EU Taxonomy

guidance.text: Organizations are aware of whether their spending and revenue are aligned with their climate transition.
guidance.textKo: Organizations are aware of whether their spending 및 매출 are aligned with their climate transition.

guidance.text:
Activity level: an economic activity can be defined as an activity through which an organization derives revenue. As described by the NACE classification system, an economic activity takes place when resources such as capital goods, labor, manufacturing techniques or intermediary products are combined to produce specific goods or services. Activity level refers to figures or information which can be provided for relevant economic activities undertaken by an organization. 
Climate transition plan: an encompassing instrument that helps organizations to align their climate ambitions. This alignment is achieved by establishing specific strategies and clear accountability mechanisms to track progress. 
Climate transition plan which aligns with a 1.5°C world : a time-bound action plan that clearly outlines how an organization will achieve its strategy to pivot its existing assets, operations, and entire business model towards a trajectory that aligns with the latest and most ambitious climate science recommendations, i.e., halving greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2030 and reaching net-zero by 2050 at the latest, thereby limiting global warming to 1.5. Please refer to the CDP Climate Transition Plan technical note for more details. 
Sustainable finance taxonomy : as defined by the Bank for International Settlements, a sustainable finance taxonomy is a set of criteria which can form the basis for an evaluation of whether and to what extent a financial asset can support given sustainability goals. The central goal of taxonomies is driving capital allocation towards sustainable activities, reducing greenwashing, and enabling simpler comparison.
guidance.textKo: Activity level: an economic activity can be defined as an activity through which an organization derives 매출. As 설명d by the NACE classification system, an economic activity takes place 경우 resources such as capital goods, labor, manufacturing techniques 또는 intermediary products are combined to produce specific goods 또는 services. Activity level refers to figures 또는 information which can be 입력 for 관련 economic activities undertaken by an organization. 기후 전환계획: an encompassing instrument that helps organizations to align their climate ambitions. This alignment is achieved by establishing specific strategies 및 clear accountability mechanisms to track progress. 기후 전환계획 which aligns with a 1.5°C world: a time-bound action plan that clearly outlines how an organization will achieve its 전략 to pivot its existing 자산, operations, 및 entire business model towards a trajectory that aligns with the latest 및 most ambitious climate science recommendations, i.e., halving greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2030 및 reaching net-zero by 2050 at the latest, thereby limiting global warming to 1.5. Please refer to the CDP 기후 전환계획 technical note for more details. Sustainable finance taxonomy: as defined by the Bank for International Settlements, a sustainable finance taxonomy is a set 기준의 which can form the basis for an evaluation of whether 및 to what extent a financial asset can support given sustainability goals. The central goal of taxonomies is driving capital allocation towards sustainable activities, reducing greenwashing, 및 enabling simpler comparison.

change.question_ko: 재무제표 내에 귀사의 기후변화계획에 부합한 비용 / 매출액을 파악하고 있습니까 ? M5. Business Strategy 1 2 3 비용 / 매출액의 기후전환계획과 부합성 파악 여부 기후전환계획과의 부합 여부를 평가하는 데 사용한 방법론 또는 프레임워크 비용 / 매출액의 지속가능한금융 분류체계와 부합 성 식별 수준 선택 항목 예 아니요 , 하지만 향후 2 년 내에 진행 예정임 아니요 , 향후 2 년 동안은 진행할 계획 없음 해당하는 항목 모두 선택 : 지속가능한금융 분류체계 기타 방법론 또는 프레임워크 선택 항목 : 전사 및 활동 단위 모두 전사 단위에서만

sectorTagsKo: 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, Food, beverage & tobacco, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: Organizations need to be aware of whether their spending 및 매출 is supporting their climate transition. Basing such an assessment on activities, projects, 또는 자산 defined as sustainable by a sustainable finance taxonomy can inform progress being made on their commitment to mitigate 및 adapt to climate change 및 enhance its credibility.
